NCT03732820.name: PROpel
NCT03732820.group: 1L mCRPC, olaparib + abiraterone vs placebo + abiraterone (rPFS HR)
NCT03732820.snippet: Source: Clarke NW et al. NEJM Evid 2022 + updates (PROpel).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 796 adults with previously untreated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (1L setting, unselected for HRR mutation status) randomized 1:1 double-blind to olaparib 300 mg BID + abiraterone 1000 mg/d + prednisone (n=399) or matched placebo + abiraterone + prednisone (n=397).

evidence.label: Primary - rPFS (Investigator-Assessed)
evidence.text: Investigator-assessed radiographic progression-free survival: median 24.8 months olaparib+abiraterone vs 16.6 months placebo+abiraterone; hazard ratio 0.66 (95% CI 0.54-0.81; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met. Benefit observed across HRR-mutated and HRR-non-mutated subgroups (HRR+ HR 0.50, HRR- HR 0.76).

evidence.label: Secondary - OS and Safety
evidence.source: Clarke 2023 Lancet Oncol + Saad 2023 Lancet Oncol
evidence.text: Interim OS HR 0.81 (0.67-1.00), trending favourable (NS at interim). Anaemia grade 3+ 15% vs 3%. Pulmonary embolism numerically higher on olaparib. MDS/AML rare. Olaparib dose reduction 20%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by metastatic site, prior taxane, and docetaxel timing. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 98% follow-up. D4 LOW - BICR for sensitivity; investigator-assessed primary with pre-specified BICR concordance. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 18 Years; max age 99 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Capable of giving signed informed consent, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the informed consent form and in the study protocol.~2. Provision of signed and dated, written informed consent form prior to any mandatory study specific procedures, sampling, and analyses.~3. For inclusion in i) the optional exploratory genetic research and ii) the optional biomarker research, patients must fulfill the following criteria:~   * Provision ...

evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: Male 399, Male 397, Male 796. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

NCT03748641.name: MAGNITUDE
NCT03748641.group: 1L mCRPC HRR+, niraparib + abiraterone vs placebo + abiraterone (rPFS HR)

allOutcomes.title: Radiographic progression-free survival (primary in HRR+ cohort, BICR, HR)

NCT03748641.snippet: Source: Chi KN et al. JCO 2023;41:3339-3351 (MAGNITUDE HRR+ cohort).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 423 adults with 1L mCRPC and HRR gene alterations (BRCA1, BRCA2, ATM, BRIP1, CDK12, CHEK2, FANCA, HDAC2, PALB2) randomized 1:1 double-blind to niraparib 200 mg + abiraterone (n=212) or placebo + abiraterone (n=211). HRR-non-mutated cohort was also randomized but futility-stopped at interim (data not included in primary pool).

evidence.label: Primary - rPFS (BICR) in HRR+ Cohort
evidence.text: BICR-assessed rPFS in HRR+ cohort: median 16.5 months niraparib+abiraterone vs 13.7 months placebo+abiraterone; hazard ratio 0.73 (95% CI 0.56-0.96; P=0.022). BRCA1/2 subgroup HR 0.53 (0.36-0.79). HRR-non-BRCA HR 0.99. Primary endpoint met in HRR+ cohort.

evidence.label: Secondary - OS and Safety
evidence.text: OS HR 0.88 (0.65-1.20; immature). Anaemia grade 3+ 30% vs 8%. Thrombocytopaenia grade 3+ 7% vs 2%. Hypertension 15% vs 13%. Niraparib dose reduction 37%; discontinuation due to AE 11% vs 8%. MDS/AML 0.7%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by HRR gene. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 95% follow-up. D4 LOW - BICR primary. D5 LOW - SAP locked; HRR- cohort futility-stopped per pre-specified rule.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* HRR gene alteration (as identified by the sponsor's required assays) as follows:~  1. Cohort 1: positive for HRR gene alteration~ 2. Cohort 2: not positive for DRD (that is, HRR gene alteration)~ 3. Cohort 3: eligible by HRR status~* Metastatic disease documented by positive bone scan or metastatic lesions on computed tomography (CT) or magnetic resonance imaging (MRI)~* Metastatic prostate cancer in the setting of castrate levels of testosterone less than or equal to (<=...

NCT03395197.name: TALAPRO-2
NCT03395197.group: 1L mCRPC (ITT, HRR-unselected primary cohort), talazoparib + enzalutamide vs placebo + enzalutamide (rPFS HR)

allOutcomes.title: Radiographic progression-free survival (primary, BICR, ITT HR)

NCT03395197.snippet: Source: Agarwal N et al. Lancet 2023;402:291-303 (TALAPRO-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 805 adults with 1L mCRPC (HRR-unselected primary ITT cohort) randomized 1:1 double-blind to talazoparib 0.5 mg + enzalutamide 160 mg (n=402) or placebo + enzalutamide (n=403). Separate HRR+ cohort also randomized (analysed separately).

evidence.label: Primary - rPFS (BICR)
evidence.text: BICR-assessed rPFS in ITT: median not reached talazoparib+enzalutamide vs 21.9 months placebo+enzalutamide; hazard ratio 0.63 (95% CI 0.51-0.78; P<0.0001). Primary met. HRR+ cohort HR 0.46 (0.30-0.70); HRR-non-mutated HR 0.70 (0.54-0.89).

evidence.label: Secondary - OS and Safety
evidence.text: OS HR 0.89 (0.69-1.14; immature). Anaemia grade 3+ 46% vs 4% - substantial; transfusions required in 35%. Talazoparib dose reduction 54%. Neutropaenia grade 3+ 18% vs 1%. MDS/AML 0.5%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 95% follow-up. D4 LOW - BICR primary. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f.
